body.title: UU root launchers: ./superclaude, ./igor, ./uu — platform-smart install + launch
body.result: igor + superclaude Python launchers at root; instance discovery (0→wizard,1→auto,>1→prompt,--id override); .ps1 Windows stubs
body.description:
A user should be able to clone UnseenUniversity, cd to the root, and run ./superclaude, ./igor, or ./uu and have everything work. Each launcher: detects OS platform, bootstraps venv if needed, reads ~/.UnseenUniversity/<device>/config.cfg, handles fresh-install prompts. Implementation hidden from user. Elevation via sudo if needed.

Instance discovery logic (no env var required):
  count(~/.UnseenUniversity/Igor-*) == 0 → run fresh-install wizard (ask for instance name, optionally DB URL)
  count(~/.UnseenUniversity/Igor-*) == 1 → use it, no prompt
  count(~/.UnseenUniversity/Igor-*) > 1 → list choices, prompt which to start
  --id flag → override discovery

These are thin root launchers that delegate to bin/superclaude and devices/igor/setup_assets/installer.py. Windows gets .ps1 equivalents.

**Affected files:** UnseenUniversity/superclaude (new), UnseenUniversity/igor (new), UnseenUniversity/uu (new), UnseenUniversity/superclaude.ps1 (new), UnseenUniversity/igor.ps1 (new), UnseenUniversity/uu.ps1 (new); devices/igor/setup_assets/installer.py (add discovery logic)
**Design rules:** platform-smart (bash + .ps1 pair), discovery-first-then-prompt, no-required-env-vars
**Scope boundary:** IN — thin launchers + discovery logic + fresh-install prompts; OUT — do not rewrite existing installer.py beyond adding discovery; Windows .ps1 files are stubs that call the bash equivalents via WSL if WSL available, else native
**Test plan:** Fresh UU clone: ./igor → wizard runs, bootstraps, launches; existing single instance: ./igor → starts without prompts; two instances present: ./igor → lists both, asks; --id flag overrides; pytest passes
